Instead `maybeScheduleDeletion` now treats a KMS "‚Ä¶ is pending deletion" response as SUCCESS (`isAlreadyPendingDeletion`): the count-suppression still blocks scheduling when a LIVE co-tenant exists, and in the concurrent both-destroyed race KMS is the dedup point ‚Äî net exactly-once, no locking, no deadlock. New PG-gated `TestDestroyKEK_ConcurrentCoTenants_ExactlyOneSchedule` (race-detector-clean; fake KMS models the already-pending dedup). | 074 | 2026-06-10 LOOM-83 (FD-25 decision) | **D-INGEST-CONSUMER-THROUGHPUT (perf).** The knowledge events consumer (`app/events/consumer.py`) processes a poll batch **sequentially** (`for msg in messages: await _handle_message`). | 066 | 2026-06-08 LOOM cycle-8 D-A2S1B2 live-smoke | **D-A2S1B2-STATUS-EFFECTS-PRODUCER-GAP ‚Äî diagnosis corrected + instrumented (LOOM-53, Cycle 11).** The cycle-8 hypothesis "`canonical_id=NULL` (anchoring) blocks the status" is **WRONG**: the Pass-2 status resolver ([pass2_writer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/pass2_writer.py) `_resolve_status_entity_id`) uses the chapter-local entity map keyed on `entity.id` (always set for any extracted entity ‚Äî NO `‚â•2 links` requirement) ‚Üí Tier-A.1 resolves a dead character that was extracted this chapter regardless of glossary anchoring. The whole producer path is **statically CORRECT** end-to-end: event-extraction prompt asks for `status_effects` (death‚Üí`gone`, example "Zhao"), the parser/`StatusEffect` model handles them, `event_order` is non-null in the full publish path, the resolver matches on `entity.id`. So cycle-8's "no `:EntityStatus`" = LLM non-compliance (didn't emit a well-formed status_effect) OR a path-specific event_order/fold miss ‚Äî **the two silent-skip points are now a metric** (`knowledge_extraction_status_effect_total{outcome}`) so the cause is observable, not guessed. 066 is independent of 065 (status write precedes `_complete_job`). **‚úÖ RESOLVED 2026-06-09 (LOOM-63 / FD-4) ‚Äî the real bug was found + fixed.** The canonical 067 run (a part-less death-chapter book, gemma) showed entities persisted but 0 `:EntityStatus`, with knowledge logs `A2-S1b: status_effect skipped ‚Äî event 'Bryn dies' has no event_order`. Root cause: `event_order` (= `chapter_base`) was derived **only** from `hierarchy_paths.chapter_index`, and the worker builds `hierarchy_paths` ONLY when `hierarchy.part is not None` ‚Üí a **flat book (chapters, no parts)** got `event_order=None` ‚Üí every `status_effect` skipped AND the dense timeline axis collapsed. Fix: thread the chapter's own `sort_order` as a separate `chapter_index` (pass2_writer fallback + persist-pass2 request field + worker client + runner capture-outside-part-gate). **Live-proven on the fixed/rebuilt stack:** part-less death-chapter ‚Üí 2 fresh `:EntityStatus{gone}` with `from_order`=chapter_sort√óstride (was 0). 5 regression tests. **Compounding cycle-8 cause also confirmed:** the original job used qwen3.6-35b (a reasoning model ‚Üí emits empty/think ‚Üí 0 status) + failed on the M1 constraint (065, cycle-10 fixed). | DONE | ‚Äî |

| 069 | 2026-06-08 LOOM cycle-13-BUILD (was LOOM-43 LOW-1) | **D-A3-REPLACE-ORPHAN-ARC-NODES (BE).** Precisely diagnosed during the planner-FE build: `outline.commit_decomposed_tree` ([services/composition-service/app/db/repositories/outline.py](../../services/composition-service/app/db/repositories/outline.py)) ALWAYS inserts a NEW `arc` node + NEW `chapter` nodes each commit, and on `replace=true` soft-archives ONLY the target chapters' SCENES (not the prior arc/chapter nodes). So every re-plan leaves the PRIOR arc node + its chapter nodes childless (their scenes archived, a fresh arc/chapters created alongside) ‚Üí orphan arc/chapter nodes accumulate in the outline. **Clean fix is BE, not FE** (the planner UI doesn't render the outline tree, so there's no FE display to filter): on `replace`, also soft-archive the prior arc + its now-childless chapter nodes for the target chapters (the recursive soft-archive idiom already exists ‚Äî outline.py:5-6). Low urgency (archived scenes already hide; the orphan nodes are inert structure). **‚úÖ RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (LOOM-78 / FD-17).** On `replace`, `commit_decomposed_tree` now archives `scene`+`chapter` nodes for all `affected` chapter_ids (also reaps pre-accumulated orphan chapter nodes) + a SCOPED childless-arc sweep over only the parent arcs of the archived chapters (`NOT EXISTS` active chapter child). **/review-impl HIGH:** the initial sweep was project-wide ‚Üí would silently archive an unrelated freshly-created empty arc; re-scoped to the affected arcs (bystander regression test added). 3 real-PG tests (full-replace ¬∑ partial-replace preserves out-of-target arc ¬∑ bystander survives); 47 integration pass. | composition-service (outline repo) | DONE |

| 053 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B BUILD subB adversary F1 (WARN) | **D-PHASE-B-EMIT-PERMANENT-FAILURE-SURFACE.** `emit_correction`'s blanket `except Exception` (correct for transient PG faults ‚Äî best-effort ¬ß6.6) also swallows PERMANENT failures (malformed payload, serialization edge, future schema drift) with only a `logger.warning`. The ¬ß10.1 replay backstop can only replay rows that made it INTO `outbox_events` ‚Äî a row whose INSERT never succeeded has zero durability. Fix: distinguish transient (pool/conn) from permanent failures; surface non-transient via a metric / error-log-at-ERROR rather than silent drop. Low risk today (payload shape is code-controlled, json.dumps(default=str)). **Empirically confirmed (session 75 smoke):** a non-UUID-coercible `aggregate_id` (a synthetic relation id) made `emit_correction` silently drop the correction (uuid.UUID() raised ‚Üí swallowed). Harmless in production (all real entity/relation/event ids are 32-hex ‚Üí UUID-coercible), but it is a concrete instance of the swallow-masks-permanent-failure concern. **‚úÖ RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (LOOM-86 / FD-19¬∑053).** `_record_emit_failure` splits the swallow: transient (`asyncpg.PostgresError`/`ConnectionError`/`OSError`/`TimeoutError`) ‚Üí warn (replay backstop applies); permanent (everything else ‚Äî non-UUID aggregate_id, malformed payload, schema drift) ‚Üí ERROR + `knowledge_correction_emit_failure_total{kind="permanent"}` (pre-seeded). Applied to both `emit_correction` + `emit_config_adjustment`. 2 new outbox tests (non-UUID‚Üípermanent+not-awaited; PostgresError‚Üítransient). /review-impl: pool-not-init‚Üípermanent accepted (anomaly-surface). | Phase E hardening (when corrections feed gold) | DONE |

A distinct FE-facing "begin-review" toggle remains optional. ORIGINAL: **Proposal lifecycle API cannot promote a fresh `proposed` proposal.** The DAG (`app/services/review.py LEGAL_TRANSITIONS`) is `proposed ‚Üí author_reviewing ‚Üí approved ‚Üí promoted`, but the API (`app/api/proposals.py`) exposes only `approve` (sets `approved` directly), `reject`, `edit` (no status change), `promote`, `retract`, `write-back` ‚Äî there is **no endpoint for `proposed ‚Üí author_reviewing`**. So `approve` from the initial `proposed` state 409s (`illegal transition proposed ‚Üí approved`), making `promote` unreachable through the API alone. Fix: add a `begin-review` endpoint (`proposed ‚Üí author_reviewing`), OR have `approve` walk `proposed ‚Üí author_reviewing ‚Üí approved` in one step. | 046 | 2026-05-31 session 100 moisture-transport model | **Fixed.** Rewrote `climate.rs::moisture_field` from **averaging** upwind neighbours to **MAX best-path** downwind-directed multi-source transport (`moisture[i] = max over upwind nbrs of moisture[nb] ‚àí land_leak ‚àí OROGRAPHIC¬∑climb`; windward-edge = off-map upwind ocean). MAX lets moisture reach inland along the wettest route from any upwind sea (multi-directional within the upwind cone) while staying wind-aware (offshore coasts dry, range rain-shadows persist). Result (seed-7 mega): interiors greener, C-group nearly doubled in the full-gradient case (equatorial spread=1: 2.2‚Üí3.9 %, `Plain` 55‚Üí98), Desert preserved 30.7 % at spread=0; bonus ‚Äî maritime cooling raised Tundra/Polar (126‚Üí375 / 5.7‚Üí11 %). Tests: `moisture_takes_wettest_upwind_path` (MAX-vs-AVG discriminator), `rain_shadow_follows_the_wind` preserved. Full lib 399 green. Residual (abundant *literal* Temperate plains) split to optional **#047 (8-zone mapping)**. Commit: (this session). |
| 045 | 2026-05-31 session 100 K√∂ppen v2 seasonality | **Fixed.** Replaced the linear insolation `lerp(28,‚àí15,lat_dist)` with a **cosine** curve (`insolation_temp`: warms mid-lat ~6.5‚Üí15 ¬∞C at 45¬∞) and rewrote `seasonal_amp` to be **continentality-gated** (`AMP_EQ + (AMP_MARITIME=4 + AMP_CONT_GAIN=24¬∑cont)¬∑lat_dist`) so maritime coasts stay low-amplitude at all latitudes. Result (seed-7 mega): Desert preserved 33.5 % at spread=0 (K√∂ppen win intact); **Tundra opened 0‚Üí126** + Polar/Boreal gradient at spread=1; the temperate C-band is now reachable (`Plain` 0‚Üí55 with Equatorial orientation). Tests: `insolation_warms_midlatitudes`, `maritime_stays_low_amplitude`, `v2_seasonality_opens_temperate_and_polar` (drives the exact build composition). Full lib 398 green. The *residual* ‚Äî Temperate stays only 1‚Äì2 % because it's a wet-mild maritime climate the single-wind moisture march under-produces ‚Äî is split out as new **#046 (moisture-transport model)**, the chosen next step. Commit: (this session). |

Closes the interleaving coverage gap between `a_long_river_gets_periodic_fords` (pure every-Nth) and `a_river_crossing_a_road...` (pure bridge). Test-only; no output change. |
| 026 | 2026-05-22 tilemap river barrier reorder | Root cause refined: **erosion** (not fill) fragments the passable region, and the river's Mountain/Lake sources/sinks required erosion first ‚Äî so the river was structurally stuck threading post-erosion narrow channels (forded most tiles). Fix: split `ObstaclePlacer` into `ObstacleSourcePlacer` (places Mountain/Lake markers on Open tiles **pre-erosion**, dual-gated per-zone + map-wide) ‚Üí `RiverPlacer` (carves the wide-open zone) ‚Üí `ObstacleFillPlacer` (erode + fill the rest post-river, skipping river Water; erosion also skips Water fords). Golden fixture river ford ratio: **~0.77 crossings ‚Üí 0.06 fords** (AC-2 gates < 0.25). Cost tradeoff accepted: continent 6.46 s ‚Üí 10.56 s (river now carves a real barrier). Strict dual `would_seal_a_gap` gate unchanged. `/review-impl` caught a HIGH (source placer needed the map-wide gate arm, not per-zone only) ‚Äî fixed + regression-tested. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md`](../specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md). |
| 029 | 2026-05-21 tilemap perf fix | `place_and_connect_object` refactored to **score-first, validate-on-demand**: cheap-filter + score all candidates (no flood fill), sort by `(score desc, flat asc)`, walk in best-first order and validate (`would_seal_a_gap` + `find_access_path`) lazily. The first sorted candidate that passes IS the winner (spec ¬ß4 proof: `first ‚àà V` of sorted `S` == `argmax_{v ‚àà V} (score, -flat)`). Naive kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as oracle. **Measurement: `treasure_placer` 973.376 s ‚Üí 2.944 s (330√ó); continent total 992.963 s ‚Üí 21.541 s (46√ó).** Golden test passes byte-exact ‚Äî no rebaseline. AC-1 oracle: 405 agreements across 3 fixtures √ó 3 templates √ó 3 optimisations √ó 3 min-distances √ó 5 sequential placements. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md`](../specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md). |
| 018 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `penrose::nearest_vertex` replaced with a bucket-based spiral search (`build_vertex_buckets` + `nearest_vertex_bucketed` over the new `UniformBuckets<Vec2>` helper in `engine/placement/spatial.rs`). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior linear scan (AC-2 oracle: 800 random queries √ó 4 vertex-field configurations incl. 1500-vertex dense; AC-3 cross-bucket tie-break by lowest index). Per-stage continent measurement: `place_zones` (Penrose + fractalize) dropped to **0.110 s** at 256¬≤ ‚Äî well under the TMP_002 ¬ß7 <500 ms budget. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 016 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` replaced with a bucket-based "any within radius" rewrite using the same `UniformBuckets<TileCoord>` helper ‚Äî 3√ó3 ring neighbourhood query (sufficiency proven: `bucket_size = ceil(sqrt(coverage_sq))` ‚áí ring-2 min dist¬≤ ‚â• 49 > 36 surface / 25 > 16 sea). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linear scan (AC-1 oracle: 96¬≤ zone √ó 5 seeds √ó 2 spans). The naive impl is kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as the oracle. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 022 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase E | Resolved by the Phase-E `RiverPlacer` design (spec D-Q5/D-Q6): the `anchor` representative point **suffices** ‚Äî no footprint extent needed. River sources are one lowest-flat-index `Mountain` obstacle anchor per mountain-bearing zone; sinks are the `Lake` obstacle anchors ‚à™ `Sea`-zone tiles. `search_path` routes to/from the anchor tile, which is added to the river search area. No additive `TilemapObjectPlacement` footprint field was required. |
| 019 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase D | Re-validated when ConnectionsPlacer landed. `seal_borders` (¬ß3.1/¬ß9) seals inter-zone borders but is not a complete wall ‚Äî passage corridors stay `Walkable` and gap-critical tiles stay `Open`. The per-zone `would_seal_a_gap` is sound regardless: every tile belongs to exactly one zone, and every passability-removing mutation (placer objects, monoliths, guards, ferries, border seals) targets an `Open` tile that is zone-interior ‚Äî so removing a zone-X tile can never split zone Y. Border-seal completeness is irrelevant to correctness; the original "border thickness" framing was the wrong lens. |

Realised land fraction is now within ¬±0.08 of target, or a documented 8192 best-effort floor (¬±0.18). Verified by `land_fraction_near_target` (16-seed sweep √ó 4 profiles). |

| 043 | 2026-05-30 C4 independent-adversary WARN-1 | **Manual single-entity name-fill does not propagate to Neo4j.** glossary `createEntity` emits an empty-name event (consumer skips it); the `PATCH /entities/{id}/attributes/{attr_value_id}` path (`attribute_handler.go patchAttributeValue`) that fills the name never calls `emitEntityUpdated`, so a manually-authored entity reaches Neo4j only when an unrelated `patchEntity` (status/tags/pin) fires.
